title: Lillian Ogata-Bonner Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454985 .

Oral history with Lillian Ogata-Bonner, who was born in the concentration camp in Tule Lake in September of 1942 and due to hardship, was placed for adoption by her birth mother. Lillian was sent to live as a baby in the Manzanar Children's Village with other Japanese American orphans and adoptable children during the incarceration. After the war, she was adopted by the Hattori family where they lived in Nevada and San Francisco. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
